rulings.Case Title: JESUS GARCIA VS. THE BEACH CHALET, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, November 03, 2025, Line 9. PLAINTIFF JESUS GARCIA's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And Paga Settlement. Plaintiff Jesus Garcia's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action And PAGA Settlement is CONTINUED TO January 20, 2026. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). Plaintiff is ordered to file a supplemental memorandum and supporting materials regarding the above no later than January 6, 2026. Any opposition or response must be filed no later than January 13, 2026. Courtesy copies (hard copy) of all filings must be delivered to the department within 48 hours of filing. Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK, N.A. VS. RYAN J WARMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, November 03, 2025, Line 10. 1 - CROSS COMPLAINANT RYAN WARMAN's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctionns. Cross-complainant Ryan Warman's motion to compel discovery responses is denied. This is a limited jurisdiction case and subject to the economic litigation rules. Under CCP 94(a), cross-complainant cannot propound more than 35 discovery requests unless the court grants a motion for additional discovery. (See CCP 95(a).) In March 2025, cross-complainant propounded his set one discovery, which improperly included 52 requests. (Burrows Decl., par. 7.) Cross-complainant subsequently propounded his set two discovery (the subject of this motion). The set two discovery contains 35 more requests and violates CCP 94(a). Cross-defendant's objection to the set two discovery based on CCP 94(a) is well-taken. The discovery is excessive and violates the economic litigation rules. Cross-complainant has now provided a "declaration of necessity," but there has been no compliance with CCP 95(a). This motion was meritless and Cross-complainant has not shown he acted with substantial justification. Cross-complainant shall pay $250 to the Clerk of the court as sanctions, forthwith but no later than December 1, 2025, and file a receipt in the docket no later than December 8, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010, 2023.030, 2030.300(d) & 2031.300(c).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: NEVILLE WRIGHT VS. TURK STREET INVESTORS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Monetary Sanctions Of $2,000.00;
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Monday, November 03, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFF NEVILLE WRIGHT's Motion For Relief From Monetary Sanctions Of $2,000.00. Plaintiff Neville Wright's Motion For Relief From Monetary Sanctions Of $2,000.00 is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Plaintiff neither timely filed nor timely served his motion. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail to place within State adds five calendar days to "any period of notice"].) see also Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Basically, in order to be heard today, a motion served by mail to an address within the state would have had to be filed and served no later than October 3, 2025 (16 court days plus 5 calendar days). Defendant, however, filed and served his motion on October 14, 2025 at the earliest--much too late. If Defendant files a new motion, he must comply with the requirements for timely filing and service. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
